content.value: The formal way to say goodbye in Cantonese is 再見 (zoi3 gin3). However, you'll more often hear people say 聽日見 (ting1 jat6 gin3) meaning 'See you tomorrow' or simply the loanword 拜拜 (baai1 baai3).

A very common social closing is 得閒飲茶 (dak1 haan4 jam2 caa4), which literally means 'When you're free, let's have tea'. Similar to 'we should do lunch sometime', it's a friendly way to end a conversation, even if no immediate plans are made.

content.translation: Let's have tea when you're free

lines.speaker: A
lines.romanization: ngo5 sin1 zau2 laa1. zoi3 gin3.

lines.speaker: A
lines.romanization: aa3 ming4, baai1 baai3! dak1 haan4 jam2 caa4 aa3!
lines.translation: Ah Ming, bye bye! Let's have tea sometime!

pages.title: Chapter 2 Complete!
pages.summary: You've successfully learned how to start and end conversations in Cantonese. You're ready to explore more topics!
